title: Powering the Gap Between Pixels and Actuators
text:
The Mission
Building Physical AI isn’t just a software challenge — it’s an infrastructure challenge. My work focuses on creating the simulation pipelines and models (RL, DL and more) that allow AI to move from the computer screen into the real world.
Your sponsorship directly impacts the ability to:

Rent High-End GPUs for large-scale Isaac Sim training.
Purchase Sensors & Actuators for “Sim-to-Real” physical validation.
Maintain Open-Source Repositories that lower the barrier for others.







NoteAny Help is appreciated given Increased Cost of Hardware and Cloud Computing

title: Powering the Gap Between Pixels and Actuators
text:
Support via Venmo

Donate via Venmo

title: Powering the Gap Between Pixels and Actuators
text:
Impact Roadmap






NoteWhat Your Funds Enable




$10: One day of cloud GPU training time.
$50: A high-precision sensor (ToF, IMU) for a physical robot prototype.
$200: A full microcontroller suite and motor drivers for a new experiment.

title: Powering the Gap Between Pixels and Actuators
text:
What’s Coming Next?
I’m currently working on a Sim-to-Real series focusing on training quadrupedal locomotion entirely in Isaac Sim and deploying to hardware. Your support helps buy the physical components needed for this deployment!

title: Renan Monteiro Barbosa
text:
Hi! My name is Renan Monteiro Barbosa.
I am a solutions Architect consultant for Robotics, Cloud (DevOPS & MLops) with a Master’s in Data Science and a foundational background in Mechanical Engineering. Specialized in pioneering Physical AI by developing end-to-end simulation pipelines, reinforcement learning algorithms, and scalable cloud architectures. Proven track record of helping NVIDIA Inception partners and global startups leverage Isaac Sim, Omniverse, and VLA models to solve complex industrial challenges. Expert in bridging the gap between virtual training and real-world robotic deployment through OpenUSD workflows, synthetic data generation, and GPU-accelerated computing.
I created Bored Engineering because most software solutions are 90% boring work of configuration, IT and building the infrastructure. Through this website I share several projects and write up random thoughts and analyses.
Please contact me at bored2engineer@gmail.com. Would love to connect about anything robotics, IsaacSim, data, potential collaborations, or just to share ideas.

  

Subscribe to my YouTube channel

title: BoredEngineer
text:
